nodes.label: zal | desire | ∅
nodes.sentence: Soon Zal desired to explore the kingdom.

nodes.label: he | set | escort
nodes.sentence: He set out with a grand escort and, after some travel, proudly entered Kabul.

nodes.label: mihrab | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: Mihrab, the ruler there and a descendant of the serpent Zohak, was wise and noble.

nodes.label: mihrab | go | ∅
nodes.sentence: Hearing that Zal, son of Sam and to whom he paid tribute, was approaching, Mihrab went out to greet him with his nobles and slaves bearing valuable gifts.

nodes.label: zal | prepare | feast
nodes.sentence: Zal, upon learning of Mihrab’s arrival, prepared a feast in his tents.

nodes.label: mihrab | din | he
nodes.sentence: Mihrab and his retinue dined with him late into the night.

nodes.label: zal | praise | beauty
nodes.sentence: After the guests left, Zal praised Mihrab’s beauty.

nodes.label: nobleman | stand | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then a nobleman stood and told him, “O Zal, you know not true beauty since you have not seen this man’s daughter.

nodes.label: face | shin | ∅
nodes.sentence: She is like a slender cypress, her face shines brighter than the sun, and her mouth is like a pomegranate blossom.”

nodes.label: heart | fill | longing
nodes.sentence: Hearing this, Zal’s heart was filled with longing, and he could not sleep, thinking of her beauty.

nodes.label: he | open | door
nodes.sentence: At dawn, he opened his court doors, and the nobles gathered according to rank.

nodes.label: mihrab | arrive | ∅
nodes.sentence: Mihrab arrived to greet him from outside the city gates.

nodes.label: zal | want | ∅
nodes.sentence: Zal wanted Mihrab to ask a favor of him.

nodes.label: mihrab | speak | ∅
nodes.sentence: Mihrab spoke: “O mighty ruler, I ask only that you stay as a guest under my roof so my heart may rejoice in your presence.”

nodes.label: zal | answer | ∅
nodes.sentence: Zal answered, “O King, do not ask this favor; it cannot be done.

nodes.label: shah | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: The Shah and Sam would be angry if they knew I dined under the roof of Zohak’s kin.

nodes.label: ∅ | ask | anything
nodes.sentence: Ask for anything else.”

nodes.label: mihrab | leave | ∅
nodes.sentence: Mihrab left sadly, bowing before Zal.

nodes.label: zal | watch | ∅
nodes.sentence: Zal watched him go and once more praised him.

nodes.label: day | pass | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then his thoughts returned to the King’s daughter and her beauty; days passed unnoticed as desire consumed him.

nodes.label: mihrab | visit | quarter
nodes.sentence: One morning Mihrab visited the women’s quarters, where he found Sindokht, his wife, and their daughter Rodabeh.

nodes.label: house | be | garden
nodes.sentence: The house was like a garden full of color and sweet scents, and the two women shone like moons of beauty.

nodes.label: mihrab | marvel | loveliness
nodes.sentence: After greeting Rodabeh, Mihrab marveled at her loveliness and blessed her.

nodes.label: sindokht | ask | mihrab
nodes.sentence: Sindokht asked Mihrab about the stranger in their city’s tents: “Tell me, what kind of man is this white-haired son of Sam?

nodes.label: mihrab | reply | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Is he worthy of our nest, or even the throne?”

Mihrab replied, “O fair cypress, the son of Sam is a hero among men.

nodes.label: heart | be | lion
nodes.sentence: His heart is like a lion’s, his strength an elephant’s.

nodes.label: he | be | crocodile
nodes.sentence: He is graceful to friends, a fierce crocodile to foes.

nodes.label: lock | add | glory
nodes.sentence: Even his white locks add to his glory, turning flaws into beauty.”

nodes.label: heart | burn | love
nodes.sentence: Hearing this, Rodabeh’s heart burned with love for Zal.

nodes.label: she | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: She could neither eat nor rest; she was like one transformed.

nodes.label: she | confide | slave
nodes.sentence: Unable to bear it alone, she confided in her beloved slaves, begging them to keep her secret and help ease her troubled heart.

nodes.label: slave | beg | she
nodes.sentence: Fearful, the slaves begged her to let go of a man marked by men and rejected by his own father, but Rodabeh would not listen.

nodes.label: wise | speak | ∅
nodes.sentence: Seeing her resolve, the wisest among them spoke: “O moon-faced beauty and slender cypress, it shall be done as you wish.

nodes.label: we | rest | ∅
nodes.sentence: We will not rest until this royal youth lies at your feet.”

nodes.label: rodabeh | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: Rodabeh was glad and promised to plant a noble tree for the faithful slave, which would bear riches, jewels, and wisdom.

nodes.label: slave | go | work
nodes.sentence: The slaves went to work, knowing their plan must be subtle.

nodes.label: they | enter | garden
nodes.sentence: Dressed in fine clothes, they cheerfully entered the garden by the river outside the city, picking roses and scattering blossoms in the water to tell fortunes.

nodes.label: they | come | tent
nodes.sentence: There they came near Zal’s tents.

nodes.label: one | say | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Zal noticed them and asked who they were; one said, “They are slaves sent by the moon of Kabul into the garden of flowers.”

Zal’s heart leapt with joy.

nodes.label: he | go | river
nodes.sentence: He went to the river with one page.

nodes.label: he | shoot | ∅
nodes.sentence: Seeing a waterbird flying up, he shot and struck it through the heart, and it fell near the rose-gatherers.

nodes.label: he | send | boy
nodes.sentence: He sent the boy to retrieve it.

nodes.label: he | be | king
nodes.sentence: Surely he is king among men.”

nodes.label: boy | answer | ∅
nodes.sentence: The boy answered, “Don’t you know the son of Sam, the hero?

nodes.label: ∅ | be | equal
nodes.sentence: There is no equal in strength or beauty.”

nodes.label: woman | object | ∅
nodes.sentence: The women objected, “Nay, boast not so.

nodes.label: house | hold | sun
nodes.sentence: The house of Mihrab holds a sun that outshines all else.”

nodes.label: boy | smile | ∅
nodes.sentence: The boy smiled and returned to Zal.

nodes.label: zal | ask | ∅
nodes.sentence: Zal asked why he smiled so, and he told him what the women said.

nodes.label: zal | send | he
nodes.sentence: Zal sent him back with gifts of pearl and gold, asking them to stay and carry jewels with their roses.

nodes.label: slave | ask | ∅
nodes.sentence: The slave who had sworn to serve Rodabeh asked to see Zal’s face, saying, “A secret known by three is no secret.”

nodes.label: zal | allow | it
nodes.sentence: Zal allowed it, and she told him of Rodabeh’s beauty.

nodes.label: passion | grow | ∅
nodes.sentence: His passion grew, and he asked her to show the way to see the maiden.

nodes.label: slave | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: The slave said, “Let us return to the women’s house.

nodes.label: we | praise | son
nodes.sentence: We will praise the son of Sam to Rodabeh and weave the net that will trap the lion in his chase of the lamb.”

nodes.label: zal | agree | ∅
nodes.sentence: Zal agreed, and the women returned, joyful, saying, “The lion is entering the snare, and the wishes of Rodabeh and Zal will come to pass.”

nodes.label: porter | scold | they
nodes.sentence: Yet, at the city gates, a porter scolded them for leaving while the stranger stayed in Kabul.

nodes.label: they | calm | anger
nodes.sentence: They calmed his anger and reached Rodabeh.

nodes.label: they | tell | she
nodes.sentence: They told her of Zal’s beauty and prowess.

nodes.label: she | smile | ∅
nodes.sentence: She smiled and said, “Why have you changed your tune?

nodes.label: you | mock | youth
nodes.sentence: You once mocked this bird-reared youth with the sage’s locks, but now sing his praises with loud voices.”

nodes.label: rodabeh | prepare | home
nodes.sentence: Rodabeh secretly prepared her home to honor a guest, adorning it with brocades from Rome and silks from India, perfumed with musk and ambergris, and blossoming with flowers.

nodes.label: all | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: As night fell, a slave came to Zal, whispering, “Come, all is ready.”

nodes.label: he | follow | she
nodes.sentence: He followed her.

nodes.label: zal | see | house
nodes.sentence: At the women’s house, Zal saw Rodabeh standing on the roof.

nodes.label: beauty | be | cypress
nodes.sentence: Her beauty was like a cypress under a full moon.

nodes.label: she | welcome | he
nodes.sentence: She welcomed him: “O young man, son of a hero, Heaven’s blessings upon you!”

nodes.label: zal | reply | prayer
nodes.sentence: Zal replied with prayers to meet her more closely, for she was above on the roof, and he below.

nodes.label: rodabeh | let | hair
nodes.sentence: Rodabeh let down her long hair, saying, “Here is a flawless rope.

nodes.label: climb | ensnare | you
nodes.sentence: Climb, O warrior, and seize my dark locks; it is fitting I ensnare you.”

nodes.label: i | become | you
nodes.sentence: Zal replied, “No, fair one; I would ill become you if I harmed you.”

nodes.label: he | kiss | hair
nodes.sentence: He kissed her hair, called for a rope, tied a running knot, threw it up, and climbed to the roof.

nodes.label: they | enter | chamber
nodes.sentence: Hand in hand, they entered the golden chambers, surrounded by slaves.

nodes.label: they | gaze | one
nodes.sentence: They gazed upon one another, recognizing great beauty, and hours passed with sweet conversation as love grew.

nodes.label: zal | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: Zal said, “O cypress, scented with musk, when Minuchihr and Sam learn this, they will be angry, accusing me of forgetting God and lifting their hands against me.

nodes.label: i | swear | ∅
nodes.sentence: But I swear this life is worthless if not spent with you.

nodes.label: i | call | heaven
nodes.sentence: I call upon Heaven to hear me: none

nodes.label: you | be | bride
nodes.sentence: but you shall be my bride.”

nodes.label: rodabeh | swear | oath
nodes.sentence: Rodabeh swore the same oath.

nodes.label: drum | sound | tent
nodes.sentence: Dawn’s drums sounded from the King’s tents, announcing the day’s arrival.

nodes.label: zal | cry | glory
nodes.sentence: Zal and Rodabeh cried together, “O glory of the world, stay a while, do not come so quickly!”

nodes.label: sun | pay | heed
nodes.sentence: But the sun paid no heed, and it was time to part.

nodes.label: zal | let | ∅
nodes.sentence: Zal let himself down from the battlements and left his beloved’s house.

nodes.label: zal | receive | greeting
nodes.sentence: When daylight filled the earth, Zal received the usual greetings from nobles and chiefs.

nodes.label: he | gather | mubid
nodes.sentence: He gathered his Mubids (wise men) and told them of his love for a daughter of the serpent lineage.

nodes.label: none | wish | ∅
nodes.sentence: The Mubids were troubled and kept silent; none wished to poison the sweetness of this love.

nodes.label: zal | urge | they
nodes.sentence: Zal urged them to speak, promising rich gifts.

nodes.label: they | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: They said a king’s honor is not hurt by a woman, and though Mihrab was descended from Zohak, he was noble and brave.

nodes.label: they | advise | zal
nodes.sentence: They advised Zal to write to his father and ask Sam to appear before the Shah.

nodes.label: zal | call | scribe
nodes.sentence: Zal called a scribe and dictated his letter.

nodes.label: he | tell | sam
nodes.sentence: He told Sam of his love and fears, reminded him of being cast out at birth, raised by a bird in a nest beneath the sun, while his father lived in silk.

nodes.label: ∅ | recall | promise
nodes.sentence: He did not defend what happened, only recalled Sam’s promise.

nodes.label: he | give | letter
nodes.sentence: He gave the letter to a messenger to ride swiftly to Sam.

nodes.label: sam | trouble | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sam was troubled when he heard.

nodes.label: woe | be | i
nodes.sentence: “Woe is me.

nodes.label: fosterling | seek | ∅
nodes.sentence: The wild bird’s fosterling seeks to unite with a cursed race.”

nodes.label: what | come | union
nodes.sentence: He pondered his reply: if he forbade Zal’s desire, he’d break his oath; if he allowed it, what could come from the union of a Deev and a bird’s son?

nodes.label: he | call | mubid
nodes.sentence: Burdened, he called his Mubids to read the stars, for blending fire and water could bring disaster.

nodes.label: they | cast | horoscope
nodes.sentence: All day they cast the horoscope for Zal and Rodabeh.

nodes.label: they | return | dusk
nodes.sentence: At dusk, they returned rejoicing.

nodes.label: spring | bring | son
nodes.sentence: To Sam they said, “Hail, Sam, for we have read the skies; A clear spring will bring a son to Zal, a hero unmatched in power and glory in Iran.”

nodes.label: heart | lift | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sam’s heart lifted.

nodes.label: he | reward | man
nodes.sentence: He rewarded the wise men and sent the messenger back to Zal with words: “I find your passion folly, son, but bound by oath, it shall be fulfilled.

nodes.label: i | go | iran
nodes.sentence: I go to Iran to plead your case to the Shah.”

nodes.label: sam | assemble | army
nodes.sentence: Sam assembled his army and set out to Iran with trumpets and cymbals heralding his arrival.

nodes.label: he | praise | god
nodes.sentence: When the messenger returned to Zal, he praised God and gave gifts to the poor and his servants.

nodes.label: soul | burn | longing
nodes.sentence: Yet no sleep touched Zal’s eyes, nor could he find rest; his soul burned with longing.

nodes.label: slave | come | letter
nodes.sentence: One day, a slave came with Sam’s letter for Rodabeh.

nodes.label: she | rejoice | ∅
nodes.sentence: She rejoiced and sent back a precious crown and ring.

nodes.label: she | meet | sindokht
nodes.sentence: On leaving her chamber, she met Queen Sindokht, who questioned her sharply about her comings and goings.

nodes.label: trembling | lie | ∅
nodes.sentence: The slave, trembling, lied that she sold jewels and robes.

nodes.label: sindokht | demand | proof
nodes.sentence: Sindokht demanded proof and found the tiara Rodabeh had embroidered in the slave’s sleeve.

nodes.label: sindokht | order | slave
nodes.sentence: Angered, Sindokht ordered the slave chained and called her daughter.

nodes.label: she | say | rodabeh
nodes.sentence: To Rodabeh she said, “O moon of noble race, blind to all but goodness, how have you strayed on evil paths?

nodes.label: man | mean | which
nodes.sentence: For which man are they meant?”

nodes.label: rodabeh | ashame | ∅
nodes.sentence: Rodabeh, ashamed, confessed all.

nodes.label: sindokht | fear | wrath
nodes.sentence: Sindokht feared the Shah’s wrath and wept alone in sorrow.

nodes.label: mihrab | enter | ∅
nodes.sentence: Mihrab entered and found her grieving.

nodes.label: she | tell | he
nodes.sentence: She told him of Rodabeh’s love for Zal.

nodes.label: heart | trouble | ∅
nodes.sentence: Mihrab’s heart troubled as well, knowing Kabul could not withstand the Shah’s might.

nodes.label: minuchihr | disturb | ∅
nodes.sentence: Minuchihr, too, was disturbed, fearing this union was Ahriman’s evil plot.

nodes.label: he | order | nauder
nodes.sentence: He ordered Nauder to summon Sam.

nodes.label: sam | come | shah
nodes.sentence: Sam came gladly before the Shah, kissing the ground and blessing him.

nodes.label: minuchihr | welcome | he
nodes.sentence: Minuchihr welcomed him, asked of the wars and the Mazinderan Deevs, and Sam told his battles.

nodes.label: shah | rejoice | valor
nodes.sentence: The Shah rejoiced at his valor, praised him, and called for a feast where heroes drank and feasted through the night.

nodes.label: court | open | dawn
nodes.sentence: At dawn, the king’s court opened for petitioners.

nodes.label: sam | come | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sam came first to speak of Zal’s love, but Minuchihr would not let him speak fully.

nodes.label: he | command | ∅
nodes.sentence: Instead, he commanded: “Go with your army.

nodes.label: ∅ | let | ∅
nodes.sentence: March on Kabul, burn Mihrab’s house, destroy the serpent’s seed; let none survive.

nodes.label: earth | rid | brood
nodes.sentence: The earth must be rid of this brood.”

nodes.label: sam | see | anger
nodes.sentence: Sam saw the Shah’s anger and knew words would not help.

nodes.label: he | bow | earth
nodes.sentence: He bowed on the earth and said, “I obey.”

nodes.label: army | advance | ∅
nodes.sentence: His army advanced, shaking the land.

nodes.label: news | bring | despair
nodes.sentence: News of Sam’s approach brought despair to Kabul.

nodes.label: house | fill | weeping
nodes.sentence: Mihrab’s house was filled with weeping.

nodes.label: zal | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: But Zal was angry and went to his father’s camp, asking for an audience.

nodes.label: sam | grant | it
nodes.sentence: Sam granted it.

nodes.label: zal | remind | sam
nodes.sentence: Zal reminded Sam of his oath, begging mercy for Kabul’s innocents.

nodes.label: heart | move | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sam’s heart moved.

nodes.label: you | speak | ∅
nodes.sentence: “You speak rightly, my son.

nodes.label: i | be | birth
nodes.sentence: I have been unjust to you since birth.

nodes.label: i | find | remedy
nodes.sentence: Hold your wrath; I will find a remedy.

nodes.label: you | bear | letter
nodes.sentence: You shall bear a letter to the Shah, and when he sees you, his heart will soften.”

nodes.label: zal | kiss | hand
nodes.sentence: Zal kissed his father’s hand and received blessings.

nodes.label: sam | write | letter
nodes.sentence: Sam wrote the letter recounting his deeds for Iran, his wars, his slaying of the dragon, and the peace he brought.

nodes.label: he | fight | ∅
nodes.sentence: Growing old, he could no longer fight, but his brave son followed him.

nodes.label: heart | consume | ∅
nodes.sentence: Yet his son’s heart was consumed by love—a longing that might kill him if denied.

nodes.label: sam | entrust | matter
nodes.sentence: Sam entrusted the matter to the Shah’s wisdom.

nodes.label: zal | set | letter
nodes.sentence: Zal set out with the letter, accompanied by the kingdom’s finest.

nodes.label: mihrab | wonder | ∅
nodes.sentence: Fear gripped Kabul; Mihrab wondered how to calm the King.

nodes.label: he | say | sindokht
nodes.sentence: He said to Sindokht, “The King is enraged because of you and Rodabeh.

nodes.label: i | lead | rodabeh
nodes.sentence: Since I cannot face him, I will lead Rodabeh to his court and kill her before his eyes.

nodes.label: wrath | ease | ∅
nodes.sentence: Perhaps his wrath will ease thus.”

nodes.label: sindokht | keep | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sindokht, quick-witted, kept silent, then asked for the treasury key.

nodes.label: ∅ | let | ∅
nodes.sentence: “Let me take what I need and go to Sam.

nodes.label: i | sway | he
nodes.sentence: Perhaps I can sway him to spare Kabul.”

nodes.label: mihrab | agree | ∅
nodes.sentence: Mihrab, distraught, agreed.

nodes.label: sindokht | leave | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sindokht left, bringing with her immense gifts: three hundred thousand gold coins, sixty silver-trimmed horses with slaves bearing cups of musk and precious stones, two hundred camels, and four elephants from India laden with brocade and carpets.

nodes.label: retinue | stretch | mile
nodes.sentence: Her retinue stretched two miles beyond Kabul’s gates.

nodes.label: sindokht | ask | guard
nodes.sentence: In Seistan, Sindokht asked Sam’s guards for entrance.

nodes.label: sam | welcome | she
nodes.sentence: Sam welcomed her, and she bowed, blessing him.

nodes.label: she | lay | treasure
nodes.sentence: She laid out the treasures, marveling at their worth.

nodes.label: sam | wonder | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sam wondered why a woman bore gifts of such value from a land so rich.

nodes.label: he | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: Accepting the gifts, he said, “Let these be given to my son’s treasurer.”

nodes.label: ∅ | ask | sam
nodes.sentence: Sindokht, encouraged, asked Sam why he wished to destroy Kabul.

nodes.label: sam | demand | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sam demanded, “Are you Mihrab’s slave or wife?

nodes.label: she | be | daughter
nodes.sentence: Is she really your daughter whom Zal saw?

nodes.label: ∅ | tell | i
nodes.sentence: Tell me of her beauty that I may judge if she is worthy of my son.”

nodes.label: sindokht | reply | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sindokht replied, “Swear to me first that you will spare my life and those dear to me.”

nodes.label: sam | take | hand
nodes.sentence: Sam took her hand and swore a solemn oath.

nodes.label: sindokht | reveal | all
nodes.sentence: Freed of fear, Sindokht revealed all: her lineage from Zohak, her marriage to Mihrab, and her daughter Rodabeh’s love for Zal.

nodes.label: she | ask | sam
nodes.sentence: She asked Sam to punish only the wicked in Kabul and spare the innocent, lest day turn to night.

nodes.label: sam | vow | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sam vowed, “By my life and oath, I shall protect you and your people.

nodes.label: zal | marry | rodabeh
nodes.sentence: Zal shall marry Rodabeh, though she be from a foreign race.”

nodes.label: he | tell | sindokht
nodes.sentence: He told Sindokht about his letter to the Shah pleading Zal’s case and that Zal was away delivering it.

nodes.label: he | plead | ∅
nodes.sentence: “Tell me of Rodabeh,” he pleaded.

nodes.label: sindokht | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sindokht said, “If Zal wishes to gladden our hearts, he must come himself and see our moon with his own eyes.”

nodes.label: all | end | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sam smiled and replied, “Rest easy; all will end as you desire.”

nodes.label: he | load | she
nodes.sentence: He loaded her with gifts and sent her away.

nodes.label: face | shine | hope
nodes.sentence: Sindokht’s face shone with hope.

nodes.label: zal | arrive | court
nodes.sentence: Meanwhile, Zal arrived at Minuchihr’s court.

nodes.label: he | kneel | ∅
nodes.sentence: He knelt and kissed the ground before the Shah.

nodes.label: minuchihr | order | musk
nodes.sentence: Moved, Minuchihr ordered musk sprinkled before him and raised him to his side.

nodes.label: zal | give | letter
nodes.sentence: Zal gave the Shah Sam’s letter.

nodes.label: king | pain | ∅
nodes.sentence: Reading it, the king was pained but promised to fulfill Zal’s wish.

nodes.label: he | ask | he
nodes.sentence: He asked him to remain a while for counsel.

nodes.label: noble | feast | ∅
nodes.sentence: The nobles and Shah feasted all night.

nodes.label: zal | present | himself
nodes.sentence: At dawn, Zal presented himself for audience.

nodes.label: man | study | star
nodes.sentence: The Shah’s wise men studied the stars for three days, then declared joyfully: “The son of Sam and daughter of Mihrab shall be a glorious pair.

nodes.label: son | be | warrior
nodes.sentence: Their son will be a mighty warrior who will expand Iran’s glory, defeat evildoers, and bring prosperity.”

nodes.label: minuchihr | command | secrecy
nodes.sentence: Minuchihr commanded secrecy.

nodes.label: he | test | wisdom
nodes.sentence: He tested Zal’s wisdom with riddles asked by the wise men.

nodes.label: zal | answer | each
nodes.sentence: Zal answered each with insight, impressing all.

nodes.label: shah | test | strength
nodes.sentence: The Shah then tested Zal’s strength.

nodes.label: he | see | ∅
nodes.sentence: Watching from his palace roof, he saw Zal outperform all in archery, spear-throwing, and wrestling.

nodes.label: minuchihr | shower | zal
nodes.sentence: The nobles cheered; Minuchihr showered Zal with gifts.

nodes.label: shah | reply | letter
nodes.sentence: The Shah replied to Sam’s letter: “O brave hero, I have seen your son and find him worthy.

nodes.label: i | grant | wish
nodes.sentence: I grant your wish and send him back to you satisfied.

nodes.label: enemy | harm | he
nodes.sentence:
May no enemy harm him.”

After receiving the king’s leave, Zal returned proudly.

nodes.label: sam | rejoice | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sam rejoiced upon receiving the letter.

nodes.label: messenger | send | mihrab
nodes.sentence: The army prepared to march; a messenger was sent to Mihrab telling him that Sam and Zal were coming to Kabul.

nodes.label: mihrab | relax | news
nodes.sentence: At the news, Mihrab relaxed and ordered his army to dress festively.

nodes.label: banner | fill | city
nodes.sentence: Silk banners and music filled the city.

nodes.label: sindokht | share | news
nodes.sentence: Sindokht shared the good news with Rodabeh, and they adorned the house as a paradise with golden carpets, ivory thrones, and scented floors.

nodes.label: mihrab | go | ∅
nodes.sentence: As the guests neared Kabul, Mihrab went out to meet them and crowned Zal with diamonds.

nodes.label: they | enter | city
nodes.sentence: They entered the city triumphantly.

nodes.label: people | bow | they
nodes.sentence: The people bowed before them.

nodes.label: sindokht | meet | they
nodes.sentence: Sindokht met them at the palace, scattering musk and jewels.

nodes.label: sam | smile | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sam, receiving their homage, smiled and asked Sindokht, “How much longer will you hide Rodabeh from us?”

nodes.label: sindokht | reply | ∅
nodes.sentence: Sindokht replied, “What will you give to see the sun?”

nodes.label: sam | answer | anything
nodes.sentence: Sam answered, “Anything you wish—my slaves and throne as well.”

nodes.label: sindokht | lead | he
nodes.sentence: Sindokht led him inside.

nodes.label: sam | strike | speechless
nodes.sentence: Sam beheld Rodabeh and was struck speechless by her beauty, beyond all dreams.

nodes.label: he | ask | mihrab
nodes.sentence: He asked Mihrab for her hand, and the marriage was arranged according to custom.

nodes.label: lover | sit | throne
nodes.sentence: The lovers sat upon a throne as Mihrab read an endless list of gifts.

nodes.label: they | feast | day
nodes.sentence: They feasted joyfully for seven days.

nodes.label: sam | return | month
nodes.sentence: After a month, Sam returned to Seistan with Zal and Rodabeh following.

nodes.label: sam | go | war
nodes.sentence: Soon Sam went again to war, leaving the kingdom to his son.

nodes.label: zal | rule | rodabeh
nodes.sentence: Zal ruled wisely beside Rodabeh, who wore a golden crown and sat as queen at his side.
